title: Operation Yashima Problems
wikitext:
[]: Operation Yashima Problems             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Nov 02, 2008 6:27 pm Yes, it was much more exciting and dramatic than what we saw in "Old Century" Evangelion , but was firing all those missiles the least bit necessary in terms of strategy, or was it all just there to add some exciting explosions? The missiles were fired to get Ramiel to expose its core so Shinji would know where to fire, correct? And perhaps also to distract Ramiel from firing upon Unit 01? That makes sense when one first thinks about it, but tons of missiles were fired before the positron rifle was even charged. This caused Ramiel to blast the missiles and all the turrets that fired them, destroying what must be millions of American dollars' worth of machinery and wasting countless explosives. And Misato just ordered them to fire more from a different location. The missile barrage was incessant and it just seems really, really excessive. Wouldn't it have been much more efficient to shoot one or two missiles at a time, starting once the positron rifle is already charged ? Ramiel didn't shoot at any of the turrets until they already fired first, so... yeah. A lot less weaponry would be destroyed, Ramiel would still expose its core when the time came that really mattered, and... you know... everything probably still would have gone down just as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Sun Nov 02, 2008 6:30 pm It was just for effect. Just something to make it seem "cool" me thinks.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Nov 02, 2008 6:33 pm What do the Japanese know of strategy? Just kidding, I wouldn't know either. Drama most likely. I suppose it's true that they could've used a lot less missles, but that would've been boring.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Sun Nov 02, 2008 6:36 pm I think with the right effects, a small number of missiles could look awesome. Though, maybe the point was to demonstrate Ramiel's ability to counter and destroy anything that's not an evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Nov 02, 2008 7:17 pm To be perfectly blunt about it, why did they actually need the evas for that fight? They could have set the gun up on some sort of hydraulic platform set to fire remotely and been just as successful.

[]: Re: Operation Yashima Problems             Quote Post by Axell » Sun Nov 02, 2008 7:52 pm NAveryW wrote: destroying what must be millions of American dollars' worth of machinery and wasting countless explosives.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Nov 02, 2008 8:29 pm The guns are simply there to try to distract the Angel for a few minutes And I'm really opposed to calling it "Old Century" Eva, because that's a term people here came up with; unless Gainax calls it that, don't; it was months before I realized that was a sarcastic reference to Gundam. It's not needed

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Nov 02, 2008 8:52 pm V wrote: The guns are simply there to try to distract the Angel for a few minutes

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sun Nov 02, 2008 10:22 pm It's sci-fi. Problems are abundant. I mean really, what organiztation would settle for having 14 year pilots as their one line of defense?

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sun Nov 02, 2008 10:30 pm NemZ wrote: To be perfectly blunt about it, why did they actually need the evas for that fight?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Nov 03, 2008 12:35 am V wrote: The guns are simply there to try to distract the Angel for a few minutes

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Mon Nov 03, 2008 6:57 am Perhaps they were collecting data or something. Or maybe it was just for teh lulz.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Nov 03, 2008 8:42 am UrsusArctos wrote: You're missing the point!

[]:             Quote Post by majlund » Mon Nov 03, 2008 9:15 am Ramiel seems to possess a very sensitive "threat detection" mechanism. Where it is lacking is in "threat response", as it seems to work on first-come first-serve manner and uses the same level of response whether dealing with a missile launcher or EVA-01. Considering that there's very little room for error in Operation Yashima it may be appropriate to "overload" Ramiel's detection system to conceal the charging positron cannon, especially given that we don't know *when* it will be considered a threat. Also the costs for rebuilding (largely unnecessary) missile launchers are probably less than that of the repair costs for an (essential) EVA.

[]:             Quote Post by simon » Mon Nov 03, 2008 9:46 am Of course they could just fire one missile from each silo, but all the saved rockets would be destroyed a moment later in a highly devastating Ramiel's blast. So why leave it to be vaporized unused and not fire it to raise the chances to hit the bastard?

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Mon Nov 03, 2008 12:36 pm V wrote: Positron rifle position is within Ramiel's firing range, and it would seem it seems it would notice the Evas if they just started sniping, so they start a massive missile barrage to distract it. As for "why not just one or two?"....that wasn't enough to distract it. Simple as that. really? that's interesting; can you point me to the source?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Nov 03, 2008 12:53 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: It's sci-fi. Problems are abundant. I mean really, what organiztation would settle for having 14 year pilots as their one line of defense?

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Mon Nov 03, 2008 1:16 pm NAW wrote: Sure!

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Mon Nov 03, 2008 2:31 pm Nerv were never very conscious of their budgetary limits. Even in 'Old Century' they just went straight to SEELE to ask for more money. When it comes to saving the world money is no object.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Mon Nov 03, 2008 7:02 pm Boy, girl, whatever. I tend to assume male whenever I don't have any reason to think otherwise. Its a bit sexist, I know, but it works out most of the time. Now about that link... Seriously, I want to know.

The missiles were fired to get Ramiel to expose its core so Shinji would know where to fire, correct? And perhaps
